LoggingIn.title: Logging In
LoggingIn.description: Any user with a valid email and password can log into Shakti and will automatically be directed to the control panel.

ResettingPassword.title: Resetting Password
ResettingPassword.description: To reset a password, follow these steps:

SigningUp.title: Signing Up
SigningUp.description: Yntraa offers a a simple and user-friendly platform for creating a subscriber cloud account, enabling access to its full range of cloud services and features. Whether you're registering as an individual or on behalf of an organization, the platform ensures a seamless sign-up experience.

Subscriber/AccountCentre/AboutOneYottaAc.title: About One Yotta Account Centre
Subscriber/AccountCentre/AboutOneYottaAc.description: The Account Centre on the Yntraa cloud console is a non-technical (commercial and support) dashboard used for viewing and managing all non-technical aspects of your Yntraa cloud account. You can access the Account Centre by clicking on any item in the user menu at the top-right, the help/support icon in the header, or the wallet icon in the header.

Subscriber/AccountCentre/InvoicesandCred.title: Invoices and Credit Notes
Subscriber/AccountCentre/InvoicesandCred.description: Invoices are generated at the end of each billing cycle for the services consumed during the billing cycle. Invoices can be accessed from the Billing > Invoices & Credit Notes section on the main navigation panel.

Subscriber/AccountCentre/Organisation-Ac.title: Organisation/Account Profile
Subscriber/AccountCentre/Organisation-Ac.description: Using the Profile section, you can manage your organisation account profile on Yntraa cloud console. The profile management section can be accessed from the Account Centre in the top helper bar by clicking on the organisation.. Account profiles include the following:

Subscriber/AccountCentre/Payments.title: Payments
Subscriber/AccountCentre/Payments.description: Yntraa cloud console supports three ways of making payments or adding money to the wallet:

Subscriber/AccountCentre/StandardAgreeme.title: Standard Agreements
Subscriber/AccountCentre/StandardAgreeme.description: Standard agreements and documents are available on the Yntraa cloud console Account Centre. Whenever any of these documents is amended or updated, you will be prompted to accept the modified documents on your next login.

Subscriber/AccountCentre/Subscriptionsan.title: Subscriptions and Services
Subscriber/AccountCentre/Subscriptionsan.description: A subscription is a record created whenever a new resource is purchased or a service is activated. In other words, a subscription is a financial ‘agreement’ that links the item that has been purchased, its value, and its renewal frequency.

Subscriber/AccountCentre/SupportandTicke.title: Support and Tickets
Subscriber/AccountCentre/SupportandTicke.description: Yntraa cloud console provides SLA-driven support to all subscriber customers in accordance with the terms and conditions outlined in the Service Level Agreement. To access the SLA, navigate to the User icon in the top helper bar and select Account under Account Centre.

Subscriber/AccountCentre/TeamandChildUse.title: Team and Child User Management
Subscriber/AccountCentre/TeamandChildUse.description: Team members or child users can be onboarded using the TEAM section from the Account Centre. Yntraa cloud console allows you to add billing, technical, or other admin users who can log in to your account and perform operations.

Subscriber/AccountCentre/UnbilledCharges.title: Unbilled Charges
Subscriber/AccountCentre/UnbilledCharges.description: All subscriptions incur prorated charges during an account’s billing cycle. These charges, also known as unbilled charges, can be accessed from Billing > Unbilled Charges in the main navigation panel.

Subscriber/AccountCentre/WalletandTransa.title: Wallet and Transactions
Subscriber/AccountCentre/WalletandTransa.description: For all practical purposes, the service balance acts as your wallet balance or purchase capacity at any given point of time. The service balance is an aggregation of your credit limit, total payments made, and the total charges incurred on your account. Your service balance is always displayed on the top bar and gets updated in real-time when a transaction or a charge is recorded.

Subscriber/Compute/Autoscale/AboutAutosc.title: About Autoscale
Subscriber/Compute/Autoscale/AboutAutosc.description: The Autoscale feature allows a system to adjust its computing resources based on the changing workload automatically. In virtual machines or instances, autoscaling helps ensure that an application or service has the right amount of resources to handle varying levels of demand efficiently. The key concept behind autoscaling is dynamic resource allocation. When demand for a particular application or service increases, autoscaling automatically adds more computing resources (such as instances or virtual machines) to handle the load. Conversely, during periods of lower demand, autoscaling reduces the number of resources to optimize cost and efficiency.

Subscriber/Compute/Autoscale/CreatingAut.title: Creating Autoscale Groups
Subscriber/Compute/Autoscale/CreatingAut.description: To create Autoscale Groups, follow these steps:

Subscriber/Compute/Autoscale/Requirement.title: Requirements for Autoscale
Subscriber/Compute/Autoscale/Requirement.description: - Yntraa now uses the native Autoscale feature of ACS. This feature is available with ACS version 4.18.0.0 onwards.

Subscriber/Compute/Autoscale/UsingStatic.title: Using Static Password In Autoscale Group’s VMs
Subscriber/Compute/Autoscale/UsingStatic.description: 1. Create a VPC and add a network tier inside the VPC.

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKuber.title: About Kubernetes
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKuber.description: Yntraa cloud console users the ability to deploy highly-available Kubernetes clusters for their cloud-native development, deployment and DevOps use cases. The Yntraa Kubernetes Service (AK8s) extends basic and advanced cluster management functionalities via the Cloud Console UI and the kubectl utility.

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKuber.title: About Kubernetes Dashboard
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKuber.description: The Kubernetes Dashboard is a web-based user interface that provides a visual representation of a cluster's resources and enables easier management and monitoring. Here's an overview of the dashboard:

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/Accessinga.title: Accessing a Cluster using the Command Line
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/Accessinga.description: Kubernetes clusters can be accessed and controlled from the command line using the kubeconfig manifest for the cluster. Each cluster has a unique manifest which is required to identify and target the cluster using the kubectl utility.

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/Accessingt.title: Accessing the Kubernetes Dashboard
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/Accessingt.description: To access the dashboard in Kubernetes version 1.24 and onwards, follow these steps:

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/ClusterOpe.title: Cluster Operations
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/ClusterOpe.description: Kubernetes clusters created on Yntraa cloud console allow for a set of management operations from the Cloud Console UI. While these options can be controlled using kubectl, these are provided on the UI for quick and easy access.

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/CreatingaK.title: Creating a Kubernetes Cluster
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/CreatingaK.description: To create a Kubernetes cluster, follow these steps:

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/IngressNet.title: Ingress Networking on Kubernetes Clusters
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/IngressNet.description: To view networking, navigate to Kubernetes and select a cluster details. This section manage a cluster's firewalls and ingress networking. Here, Port forwarding and load balancing rules can also be managed using kubectl.

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/Overview.title: Overview
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/Overview.description: To view the below details, navigate to Kubernetes, select a Kubernetes Cluster, and access the Overview tab.

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/ScalingKub.title: Scaling Kubernetes Clusters
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/ScalingKub.description: Yntraa cloud console allows for manual and automatic cluster scaling. Clusters scaling can be configured from the Nodes section of cluster details or from the Operations section of cluster details.

Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/ViewingKub.title: Viewing Kubernetes Cluster Details
Subscriber/Compute/Kubernetes/ViewingKub.description: Yntraa cloud console offers a detailed view of Kubernetes clusters on the UI. Yntraa also brings the full power and accessibility of cluster management via the kubectl interface.

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/AboutL.title: About Linux Instances
Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/AboutL.description: Yntraa cloud console offers a highly usable and visual way of working with and operating Linux Instances using the Cloud Console. You can access all the Linux Instances available in your account in the following way:

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/AppOve.description: The following sections list the instructions to verify/login into DB applications:

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Config.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to the Operating Linux Instances, and select Linux Instance and access the Alerts tab.

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Connec.title: Connecting to a Linux Instance
Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Connec.description: You can connect to Linux Instances via SSH sessions using passwords (less secure) or SSH key pairs (most secure)

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Creati.title: Creating Linux Instances
Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Creati.description: Before creating a Linux instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the Linux Instances.

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Networ.description: To view the networks of particular Instance, navigate to Operating Linux Instances, select a Linux Instance and access the Networking tab.

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Operat.description: To view all available Instance operations, navigate to the Operating Linux Instances, select a Linux Instance and access the Operations tab.

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Protec.title: Protecting Linux Instances
Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Protec.description: Enabling protection ensures that all instances are protected while all the threats are tracked.

Subscriber/Compute/LinuxInstances/Workin.description: To view all the Snapshots taken for Instance, navigate to Operating Linux Instances, select a Linux Instance and access the Snapshots tab.

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/AboutRH.title: About RHEL Instances
Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/AboutRH.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) Instances are virtual machines that run the images of RHEL Operating System.

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Configu.title: Configuring Alerts
Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Configu.description: Alerts get triggered whenever a configured condition is met. You can create multiple alerts on an instance. Alerts are sent to recipients that you can define and manage.

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Connect.title: Connecting to a RHEL Instance
Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Connect.description: You can connect to RHEL Instances via SSH sessions using passwords (less secure) or SSH key pairs (most secure)

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Creatin.title: Creating RHEL Instances
Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Creatin.description: Before creating a RHEL instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the RHEL Instances.

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Network.title: Networking Management
Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Network.description: To view the networks of a particular Instance is attached to, navigate to  RHEL Instances, and access the Networking tab.

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Protect.title: Protecting RHEL Instances
Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Protect.description: Enabling protection ensures that all instances are protected while all the threats are tracked.

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Reconfi.description: To view available reconfiguration options, navigate to RHEL Instances and access the Reconfigure tab.

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/Viewing.description: Yntraa cloud console offers a highly usable and visual way of working with and operating RHEL Instances using the Cloud Console. All RHEL Instances available in your account can be accessed in the following way:

Subscriber/Compute/RHELInstances/VolumeM.description: To view the disks attached to particular Instance, navigate to RHEL Instances and access the Volumes tab.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Abou.title: About Windows Instances
Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Abou.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Windows Instances are virtual machines that run the images of Windows Operating Systems.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Conf.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Alerts tab .

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Conn.title: Connecting to a Windows Instance
Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Conn.description: Unlike Linux Instances that use SSH connections, Windows Instances use the remote desktop protocol (RDP).

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Crea.title: Creating Windows Instances
Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Crea.description: Before creating a Windows Instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the Windows Instances.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Netw.description: To view the networks that a particular Instance is attached to, navigate to the Windows Instances Screen, select a Windows Instance, and access the Networking tab.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Prot.description: Enabling protection ensures that all instances are protected while all the threats are tracked.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Reco.description: To view available reconfiguration options, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance, and access the Reconfigure tab.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/View.description: To view the details of Windows Instances, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Overview tab.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/View.title: Viewing Graphs and Utilisation of Windows Instances

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Volu.description: To view the disks attached to this instance, navigate to Windows Instances Screen, select a Windows Instance, and access the Volumes tab.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Wind.title: Windows Instance Operations
Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Wind.description: To view all available Instance operations, navigate to the Windows Instances Screen, select a Windows Instance, and access the Operations tab.

Subscriber/Compute/WindowsInstances/Work.description: To view all the snapshots taken for the Instance, navigate to Windows Instances Screen, select a Windows Instance, and access the Snapshots tab.

Subscriber/Networking/DNSManagement/Abou.title: About DNS Management
Subscriber/Networking/DNSManagement/Abou.description: Apiculus servers can be used as authoritative name server(s) for managing DNS records. If the Service is enabled, Cloud Console users can bring their own domains (BYOD) to use with the workloads created using Apiculus-powered clouds.

Subscriber/Networking/DNSManagement/Link.title: Linking Domains to Apiculus Cloud Console
Subscriber/Networking/DNSManagement/Link.description: The Apiculus-powered cloud has nameservers if the service provider has enabled this service. These name servers are simply fully-qualified domain names (FQDN) for the servers that store all the domain/zone information. Typically, these name servers will be in the following format:

Subscriber/Networking/DNSManagement/Mana.title: Managing DNS Records
Subscriber/Networking/DNSManagement/Mana.description: DNS records and subdomains can be managed on Apiculus Cloud Console to be used with Instances, IPv4 Addresses and other workloads created on Apiculus-powered clouds. This only requires a domain to be linked with the Apiculus nameservers.

Subscriber/Networking/DNSManagement/Swit.title: Switching to Another DNS Provider
Subscriber/Networking/DNSManagement/Swit.description: In case you don't want to use the DNS Management Service on your Apiculus-powered cloud, you can always switch back to another provider of choice. To do so, head to your domain name provider's control panel and update the nameserver details to those of the DNS provider you wish to use.

Subscriber/Networking/IPv4-basedNetworki.title: About IPv4-based Networking
Subscriber/Networking/IPv4-basedNetworki.description: IPv4 Addresses form the backbone of networking operations on Apiculus. Internally, IPv4 Addresses are treated as a monetisable Service on Apiculus. However, the use of IPv4 Addresses is implicit as part of other essential Services.

Subscriber/Networking/IPv4-basedNetworki.title: Establishing Site-to-Site VPN Between Two VPCs
Subscriber/Networking/IPv4-basedNetworki.description: A Site-to-Site Virtual Private Network (VPN) provides a secure and encrypted connection between two Virtual Private Clouds (VPC), enabling seamless communication across geographically separated networks. This setup is commonly used to connect different environments (such as production and development) or extend on-premises infrastructure to the cloud.

Subscriber/Networking/IPv4-basedNetworki.title: Managing VPN Gateways and Site-to-Site VPN
Using Remote Access VPN with VPC
Subscriber/Networking/IPv4-basedNetworki.description: To use a VPN client to connect to your VPC, follow these steps:

Subscriber/Networking/L2 Networks/AboutL.title: About L2 Networks
Subscriber/Networking/L2 Networks/AboutL.description: L2 networks, often called data link layer networks, play a crucial role in computer networking by establishing direct connections between devices in a local network and ensuring data packets are delivered accurately to their intended destination.

Subscriber/Networking/L2 Networks/AddNew.title: Adding New L2 Network
Subscriber/Networking/L2 Networks/AddNew.description: To add a new L2 network, click the New Network button. The following screen appears:

Subscriber/Networking/LoadBalancerInstan.title: About Load Balancer Instances
Subscriber/Networking/LoadBalancerInstan.description: The Apiculus Load Balancer Instances (LBI) Service allows Cloud Console users to create load balancer appliances, which are Linux Instances pre-loaded with a Citrix NetScaler VPX image. LBI can be used with VPC networks to achieve various traffic management features that are not possible using the standard VPC-based load balancing.

Subscriber/Networking/LoadBalancerInstan.title: Activating the NetScaler VPX Control Panel
Subscriber/Networking/LoadBalancerInstan.description: The following steps can be used for activating the NetScaler VPX control panel and accessing it after activation:

Subscriber/Networking/LoadBalancerInstan.title: Creating a Load Balancer Instance
Subscriber/Networking/LoadBalancerInstan.description: Load Balancer Instances can be created inside a VPC by following these simple steps:

Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/AboutN.title: About NAT Gateways
Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/AboutN.description: A Network Address Translation (NAT) gateway is a networking component in cloud and on-premises environments that enables resources within a private subnet (such as virtual machines or containers) to connect to the internet or other external networks, without exposing these resources directly to incoming connections from those external networks. This helps in enhancing security in cloud infrastructure by keeping resources in private subnets isolated from direct public access.

Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.title: Instances
Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.description: This section lists all the instances that are a part of this Virtual Firewall.

Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.title: IPv4 Addresses
Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.description: Each virtual gateway comes with a default IPv4 address that belongs to the Virtual Gateway governing the network. The default (source - NAT) IPv4 address can be used to access the Virtual Gateway publicly or via Remote Access (RA) VPN.

Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.title: Operations
Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.description: You can perform the following operations in the Operations section:

Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.title: Overview
Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.description: Navigate to the Overview tab to view the following details:

Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.title: Viewing and Managing NAT Gateways
Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/Managi.description: To manage a NAT gateway, follow these steps:

Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/PortFo.title: Port Forwarding for VM via VNF
Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/PortFo.description: This section outlines the steps to configure port forwarding for a Virtual Machine (VM) using a Virtual Network Function (VNF) in a cloud environment. Port forwarding allows external clients to securely access services (for example SSH, HTTP) hosted on the VM by mapping ports from the VNF’s public IP to the VM’s private IP. This approach improves network segmentation and control by routing all incoming traffic through the VNF layer.

Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/PortFo.title: Port Forwarding with Public IP
Subscriber/Networking/NATGateways/PortFo.description: In Apiculus cloud platform, an additional Public IP enables you to expose internal services hosted on virtual machines (VMs) to the internet by creating dedicated port forwarding rules. This approach is especially useful when multiple external IPs are needed for services such as SSH, HTTP, or custom applications. By assigning specific public IPs to particular services or VMs, you can achieve greater control, flexibility, and network segmentation.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Ac.title: Accessing the VFI Control Panel
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Ac.description: The Virtual Firewall Service is delivered as an integration using OS images pre-loaded with pfSense or FortiGate VM.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Ad.title: Adding Instances to a VFI-based Network
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Ad.description: Instances can be created and placed in the L2-LAN (Free Networks) created by the Virtual Firewall. These Instances use special Linux OS images, which are available as a separate OS Collection. Users can create Linux Instances, and choose the appropriate options/controls to create an Instance in the Virtual Firewall network.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Cr.title: Creating a Virtual Firewall
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Cr.description: To create a Virtual Firewall, follow these steps:

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/De.title: Deploying VNF and VM Within VNF
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/De.description: This section provides a step-by-step guide for deploying a Virtual Network Function (VNF)—specifically a virtual firewall—and provisioning a Virtual Machine (VM) behind it using the Apiculus Cloud platform. This setup allows users to route VM traffic through the VNF, enabling secure and segmented network environments.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: About Firewall Instances
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: Virtual Firewalls enables you to use Virtual Firewalls in a Virtualized Network Function (VNF) style, which include support for multiple VLANs and Public IPv4 addresses and automated service activation. This allows for more robust firewall management capabilities.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Configuring Alerts
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Alerts tab.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Networking Management
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: To view the networks that of particular Instance, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Networking tab.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Operations
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: To view all available Instance operations, navigate to the Networking, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Operations tab.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Overview
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: To view the below details, navigate to the Firewall Security, select the Virtual Firewall and access the Overview tab.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Reconfiguring Virtual Firewall
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: To view available reconfiguration options, navigate to the Networking section and select a Virtual Firewall and access the Reconfigure tab.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Snapshots
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: To view all the Snapshots taken for Instance, navigate to the Networking, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Snapshots tab.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Viewing Graphs and Utilization
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: To view the available graphs and monitor the instance in real-time, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Graphs tab.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Viewing Virtual Firewall Details
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: As the virtual firewalls are created, Cloud Console users can view the enabled Virtual Firewalls in the Networking > Virtual Firewalls section. The following details are shown:

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.title: Volume Management
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualFirewall/Fi.description: To view the disks attached to this Instance, navigate to the Networking, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Volumes tab.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.title: About Virtual Private Clouds
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.description: Details about Virtual Private Cloud operations and actions can be found in their respective sections.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.title: Creating VPC Subnets and Tiers
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.description: VPCs follow the convention of 3-tiered architectures, with web, app, and DB tiers forming the norm. You can, however, configure these tiers to suit your application architecture or just follow the common convention.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.title: IPv4 Addresses and VPC
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.description: IPv4 Addresses are an integral part of using VPC networking, and need to be used to access various components of the VPC. By default, a public IPv4 Address is assigned to the VR which can communicate through the internet to transmit traffic to/from the VR. This IPv4 can also be used for configuring remote access (L2TP) and site-to-site (IPSec) VPN connections.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.title: Managing Access Control on VPC Subnets
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.description: Access control policies can be created using Access Control Lists (ACL) and configuring rules within these ACL (called ACL Rules). An ACL can then be applied to any tier within the VPC. These policies govern what traffic is allowed or restricted into and from your VPC network tiers.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.title: Overview
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.description: To view a list of sections and the various operations or actions you can perform by going inside the particular section, click on the VPC name. Below the VPC name is an informational view where you can find the details below.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.title: Reconfiguring a VPC
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.description: The Reconfigure section/tab lists your current subscription details and allows you to reconfigure the networking pack or switch between hourly and monthly pricing.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.title: VPC Management and Basic Operations
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.description: VPC management offers the following operations. These are basic VPC management actions and don't have any impact on the actual network configurations.

Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.title: Difference Between L2 Networks and VPC
Working with VPN Connections in a VPC
Subscriber/Networking/VirtualPrivateClou.description: The VPC feature on Apiculus provides advanced networking capabilities for use with VPN gateways and connections over an IPSec tunnel. Apiculus gives the ability to create virtual private networks (VPN) to access virtual machines inside a VPC.

WalletandTransactions/WalletandTransacti.title: Wallet and Transactions
WalletandTransactions/WalletandTransacti.description: The Wallet and Transactions section gives users a quick view of their account balance and transaction history. It shows available funds, including wallet and voucher balances, and offers basic tools to search, filter, and download transaction records. This section helps users keep track of their billing and payments in one place.
